install_tell: fx director start (or fx director start --disable-tls)
misconfig_patterns: mTLS-on by default (use_tls=True, require_client_auth=True) — strongest auth-on-default in Cat-53
require_client_auth is ignored when use_tls=False — no 'TLS without client cert' middle ground exists by design
Insecure escape hatch documented: fx director start --disable-tls, Federation(...) kwarg, use_tls=False
Release notes mention earlier bug where TLS-on was occasionally broken in distributed setups — operators may have shipped --disable-tls as a permanent workaround
Upstream Open Federated Learning repo has an ARCHIVE NOTICE pointing community at Flower — population may be aging / unmaintained

active_probe.false_positive_check: Empty reflection = cert-required (auth-on); populated reflection = insecure mode (finding)

deployment_tells: fx pki wraps smallstep step-ca (downloaded by OpenFL itself) — step-ca on 8443 next to gRPC on 50051 is the strong co-deploy signal
Aggregator port plan-defined (50051/50052 in tutorials, no single canonical default)
gRPC-over-mTLS — HTML/body/header facets return zero; route to Censys + Stage 0c scanner
FeTS / BraTS (Penn + Intel, 71 hospital sites, six continents), pharma, insurance, ISS
pivot_paths: Direct-IP TLS probe (no SNI) on 50051 -> default cert leak = operator FQDN
Censys SAN extraction on 8443 step-ca cert -> consortium identifier
vulnerabilities: Zero direct CVEs / GHSAs against securefederatedai/openfl
Sibling-class risk: NVFlare had unauth pickle-deserialization RCE — class-of-bug worth probing on OpenFL, no public audit
